title: Understand: Presidency—The Electoral College
description: Check your understanding of the the Electoral College with this set of multiple-choice questions.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Who referred to the president as “the steward of the public welfare” in his “New Nationalism” speech?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which president, who later became chief justice of the United States, argued that the people should be able to carry out their will only after the “wholesome delay and deliberation” promoted by the structure of the Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**According to Justice Robert Jackson, the president has the greatest power when acting \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: when the Supreme Court is not in session

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the total number of electors, and thus the total number of votes, in the Electoral College?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Technically, Americans who cast their ballots on Election Day are determining \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: who will be the winner of the presidential election
which electors will be appointed to the Electoral College in each state
who will be in the president’s cabinet

assessment_items.question_content:
**To win a presidential election, a candidate must receive \_\_\_\_ electoral votes.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Today, people debate whether the U.S. should keep or eliminate the Electoral College. Constitutionally, formally eliminating the Electoral College would require \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**During a presidential election, if no candidate wins a majority in the Electoral College, then the winner is decided by \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a  majority of the state governors

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which president holds the record for issuing the most executive orders?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What topic is discussed in Article II, Section 1 of the Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Qualifications for voters in congressional elections
Selection of U.S. senators by state legislatures
The legislature’s role in determining the time, place, and manner of holding elections
The Electoral College system of electing the president

assessment_items.question_content:
**The contested election of 1800 resulted in a tie between which two candidates?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which group’s votes directly determine who will be president of the United States every four years?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
